name: -h
options.description: Print help and exit

options.name: -a
options.description: Causes list selections to be ANDed

options.name: -A
options.description: Use alternative name list file

options.name: -b
options.description: Avoid kernel functions that might block: lstat, readlink, stat

options.name: -c
options.description: Select listing for processes whose command begins with c (^ to negate, /c/[bix] for regex)

options.name: +c
options.description: Set COMMAND column width (default 9)

options.name: -C
options.description: Do not report any pathname component from kernel's name cache

options.name: -D
options.description: Control use of the device cache file

suggestions.name: u
suggestions.description: Read and update the device cache file

options.name: +d
options.description: Search for all open instances of directory s and the files and directories it contains at its top level

options.name: -d
options.description: Specifies a list of file descriptors to exclude (^) or include in the output listing

options.name: +D
options.description: Search for all open instances of directory D and all the files and directories it contains to its complete depth (SLOW)

options.name: +E
options.description: Display endpoint info and files

options.name: -E
options.description: Display endpoint info

options.name: -F
options.description: Select output fields for other programs to parse (-F? for help)

options.name: +f
options.description: Enable path name-based kernel lookups for file system names

options.name: -f
options.description: Disable path name-based kernel lookups for file names

options.name: -g
options.description: Exclude (^) or select and print process group IDs

options.name: -i
options.description: Select listing of IPv[46] files (format: [46][proto][@host|addr][:svc_list|port_list])

options.name: -K
options.description: List or ignore tasks (kernel threads)

options.name: -l
options.description: Convert UIDs to login names

options.name: +L
options.description: List link counts below the specified number

options.name: -L
options.description: Suppress link count listing

options.name: +m
options.description: Use or create mount supplement file

options.name: +M
options.description: Enable portMap registration

options.name: -M
options.description: Disable portMap registration

options.name: -n
options.description: Do not convert network numbers to host names

options.name: -N
options.description: Select listing of NFS files

options.name: -o
options.description: List file offset or set offset digits

args.name: o
args.description: Number of decimal digits for file offset (default 8)

options.name: -O
options.description: Bypass overhead avoiding strategy (RISKY)

options.name: -P
options.description: Do not convert port numbers to port names

options.name: -p
options.description: Exclude (^) or select processes by PID

options.name: -Q
options.description: Allow failed search to occur without error

options.name: -R
options.description: Print parent PID (PPID)

options.name: +r
options.description: Repeat listing every t seconds until no open files found (format: t[m<fmt>])

options.name: -r
options.description: Repeat listing every t seconds forever (format: t[m<fmt>])

options.name: -s
options.description: List file size or exclude (^)/select protocol states by name (format: p:s where p is TCP or UDP)

options.name: -S
options.description: Set stat timeout in seconds (default 15)

options.name: -t
options.description: Produce terse output (PIDs only, no header)

options.name: -T
options.description: Disable TCP/TPI info or select Fl, Q, St info (format: fqs)

options.name: -U
options.description: Select listing of Unix domain socket files

options.name: -u
options.description: Exclude (^) or select by login name or UID

options.name: -v
options.description: List version information

options.name: -V
options.description: Indicate unsuccessfully searched-for items

options.name: +w
options.description: Enable warnings

options.name: -w
options.description: Suppress warnings

options.name: -X
options.description: Skip reporting of TCP, UDP, and UDPLITE files

options.name: -x
options.description: Cross over file systems or symbolic links when using +d or +D

args.name: fl
args.description: f to cross file systems, l to cross symbolic links

options.name: -Z
options.description: Specify SELinux security context

args.name: names
args.description: Named files or file systems to select
